checks.finding: Field Definitions (stable title marker, capability refresh, freshness-check, current-generation) are concrete and unambiguous. One narrow non-blocking clarification remains: AC-006/REQ-002 never state how the dedicated fetch-unavailable tracking issue is first provisioned, and TEST-006's fixtures assert 'ZERO issue-create calls' across all three fetch-failure scenarios, implying an existing issue is always pre-seeded -- the very-first bootstrap path is untested. This does not create a tested-behavior mismatch (every tested branch is pinned to search+comment-only), so it does not clear the FAIL evidence bar; a reasonable design-time resolution (human-provisioned tracking issue, mirroring AC-011's human-copy pattern) is inferable without inventing product behavior.

No direct conflicts found across requirements/AC/constraints/non-goals. The fail-soft (fetch failure) vs. no-bypass (genuine drift) distinction is explicitly disambiguated in Edge Cases; the protected-file carve-out is scoped to exactly one line (.github/workflows/test.yml registration) and reconciled consistently across Overview, AC-011, Roles and Permissions, and Security Boundary B3; v1/v2 registry write boundaries (Non-goals, Security Boundary B2, AC-013) are consistent throughout.

Round-3's TEST-006 rewrite (three fetch-failure scenarios: both-fail, Anthropic-fail/OpenAI-succeed, OpenAI-fail/Anthropic-succeed, each asserting no partial-data divergence is computed) closes round-2's Major gap. Remaining material paths are covered: TEST-007 (divergence + dedup negative branch), TEST-020 (no-diff/zero-side-effect), TEST-021 (adversarial-payload trust boundary), TEST-013 (v1 byte-identical hygiene), TEST-011 (protected-file fail-closed staging with explicit no-fallback design). Scraping/parse-degradation false positives are explicitly scoped to human triage in Non-goals rather than left unaddressed.

All 5 Open Questions (OQ-001..OQ-005) are marked RESOLVED with concrete resolution text. All 4 listed Assumptions (C1 schema shape, self-improvement-pr-guard denylist scope, guard_invariants protected_gate_suffixes membership, assert_literal fixed-string matching) carry explicit re-verify-at-implementation-time or harmless-if-changed fallbacks rather than being left as open, blocking decisions.

The human-copy governance boundary for the protected .github/workflows/test.yml registration line (AC-011/TEST-011) is fully testable: staged directory + MANIFEST.sha256 hash comparison, a live-file diff assertion at staging time, and an explicit fail-closed design (PR CI stays red until the human's pre-merge commit lands, 'no staged-candidate fallback or other special case' per Edge Cases). No bypass path is offered anywhere in the spec.

Field Definitions, Security Boundaries (B1-B4), Roles and Permissions, Main Workflows (with explicit External vs. in-spec Blockers per task), and Edge Cases collectively leave no missing product-behavior decision for a design reviewer to invent. Round-3's edit closes the last identified Major gap without introducing new AC/TEST drift (21 AC/TEST pairs remain 1:1, requirements.md unchanged per precheck-result.json's edit_summary).
